error_signature: The filename, directory name, or volume label syntax is incorrect.
title: Error executing command via SSH due to incorrect path syntax
symptom: When attempting to run a batch file via SSH, n8n returns an error indicating that the filename or directory name syntax is incorrect.
applies_when: Using SSH node to execute commands
Running a batch file on a remote Windows server
not_this_if: The command executes successfully without errors
root_cause: The error occurs because the path to the batch file is not correctly specified in the SSH command. Using a fixed path may lead to syntax issues, especially if the path contains spaces or special characters.
fix_steps: Change the path from a fixed string to an expression in the SSH node settings
Enter the command with the desired path to the batch file
Save the changes and try executing the command again
agent_instruction: Modify the SSH command to use an expression for the path and ensure it is correctly formatted.
